name: 114 Faubourg
address: Le Bristol, 114 rue du Faubourg-Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris, France
description: Located inside Le Bristol in Paris’s 8th arrondissement, 114 Faubourg offers a polished brasserie setting with a striking interior of gilded columns, glowing orange walls and a dramatic staircase leading to the lower dining room. The atmosphere balances energy and intimacy, with open kitchens adding to the sense of theatre. Its cooking takes a discreetly original approach to French classics, with careful presentation and well-judged flavour combinations. This is a strong choice for diners who enjoy refined brasserie dining with a contemporary touch in an elegant, lively setting.

name: Accents Table Bourse
description: Accents Table Bourse offers contemporary fine dining in a Scandinavian-styled setting in Paris’s 2nd arrondissement. Chef Romain Mahi’s cooking blends classic technique with more adventurous touches, including roasted and smoky accents, while the menu is described as balanced and precise from start to finish. Desserts are a particular strength, with light, carefully crafted compositions. The restaurant’s warm, vibrant service adds to the experience. It should appeal to diners looking for a polished Michelin-starred meal that combines refinement, subtle experimentation, and a modern atmosphere.

name: Agapé
description: Agapé is a Michelin-starred modern restaurant in Paris’s 17th arrondissement, set in a cosy, welcoming room with a soft, understated palette. The kitchen focuses on premium seasonal ingredients, shaped by chef Yoshi Nagato and complemented by desserts from Asuka Ishiba. Dishes may include Brittany lobster, Challans duckling with honey and Sichuan pepper, or roast sweetbread with girolles, presented with precise, stylish plating and generous portions. A thoughtful selection of organic and natural wines adds to the appeal. It should suit diners looking for refined cooking in an intimate, relaxed setting.

name: Aida
description: Aida is a discreet, intimate Japanese restaurant in Paris 7e, with a minimalist white interior and just a handful of counter seats overlooking the teppanyaki griddles. The single tasting menu highlights precise, understated cooking that draws a clear line between Japanese technique and French ingredients. Dishes such as sashimi, Brittany lobster, chateaubriand, and sweetbread are prepared with careful slicing, seasoning, and grilling, always keeping the main ingredient in focus. It should appeal to diners who value refinement, close-up counter service, and a calm, elegant setting.

name: Akrame
description: Akrame is a contemporary, Michelin-starred restaurant near La Madeleine that presents an inventive, ingredient-led cuisine in a discreet setting. The dark, fashion-forward dining room creates a sleek, intimate atmosphere, while the kitchen focuses on carefully crafted dishes and bold tasting menus. The experience is especially suited to diners who enjoy surprise-driven meals and a modern, creative approach to fine dining. With its emphasis on first-class produce and imaginative combinations, it offers a polished, distinctive Parisian address for adventurous guests.

name: Alan Geaam
description: Alan Geaam offers a refined Lebanese-influenced tasting of French fine dining, shaped by a chef who blends his Paris training with flavors from Lebanon. The kitchen is known for original recipes that bring together French culinary heritage and subtle Middle Eastern touches, creating a distinctive cross-cultural style. As a Michelin-starred address, it suits diners looking for an elegant meal with a personal story behind it and a menu that reflects both craftsmanship and ambition. Expect a polished, serious experience rather than a casual neighborhood restaurant.

name: Aldehyde
description: Aldehyde is an intimate, one-star dining room near the Seine where Tunisian-born chef Youssef Marzouk works in an open kitchen-bar. The evening experience centers on a single surprise tasting menu that blends French technique with North African influences. Dishes are carefully plated and show a restrained hand with spices, herbs, and citrus, allowing the ingredients to stay in focus. With its compact setting and precise cooking, the restaurant will appeal to diners who enjoy refined, creative menus and a personal, chef-led atmosphere.

name: Alliance
description: Alliance is a two-star French restaurant in Paris’s 5th arrondissement, where a small dining room and a close-knit team create an intimate experience. The cooking favors clarity and restraint over ornament, with a focus on fine seasonal produce and surprise menus tailored to the room’s few tables. Dishes are described as subtle yet precise, with a balance of visual appeal and strong flavor. The pared-back interior includes discreet Japanese touches and a view into the kitchen, while polished, gracious service adds to the sense of occasion. It should appeal to diners who enjoy refined, ingredient-led tasting menus in a snug setting.

name: Alléno Paris au Pavillon Ledoyen
description: Set in a handsome neoclassical building in Paris’s 8th arrondissement, this three-star restaurant offers a polished, classic French fine-dining experience. Large windows, elegant table settings, and highly coordinated service create a formal yet welcoming atmosphere. Yannick Alléno’s cooking is known for its technical precision and modern approach, with particular emphasis on refined jus and sauces that support the menu’s French foundation. It is a strong choice for diners seeking a luxurious setting and a highly crafted tasting experience that balances tradition with contemporary technique.

name: Amâlia
address: 32 rue de la Fontaine-au-Roi, 75011 Paris, France
description: Amâlia is a Michelin-starred Italian-led restaurant in Paris’s 11th arrondissement that presents a refined, classic setting with tablecloths and polished service. The cooking leans toward a modern take on the French repertoire, shaped by Italian touches and other influences, with a clear emphasis on precision and bright, acidic flavors. Dishes may include inventive combinations such as smoked veal tartare with citrus, cashew and sea urchin, or red mullet with a rich sauce. It should appeal to diners who enjoy elegant fine dining with technical cooking and a contemporary, cross-cultural sensibility.

name: Anne
description: Set in the elegant Pavillon de la Reine on Place des Vosges, Anne offers a refined contemporary dining experience in a plush, sophisticated room, with an inviting patio for fair-weather meals. The kitchen leans toward polished French cooking, with dishes such as golden grey mullet carpaccio and monkfish à la meunière, alongside a standout oeufs à la neige dessert. Its appeal lies in the combination of historic surroundings, calm atmosphere, and carefully executed plates. It should suit diners looking for a stylish, quietly luxurious meal in central Paris.

name: Anona
description: Anona is a Michelin-starred contemporary restaurant in Paris 17e, set in the Batignolles area. The cooking leans toward sustainable, often plant-forward gastronomy, with an emphasis on regional produce and inventive presentations. By day, guests can choose from a set menu and a few à la carte dishes; in the evening, a multi-course tasting menu sets the tone. The atmosphere suits diners looking for a polished but unpretentious experience, whether for a business lunch or a more intimate dinner.

name: Apicius
description: Apicius is a one-star French restaurant set in an elegant 18th-century private mansion near the Champs-Élysées. Its refined rooms and terrace create a calm, almost garden-like escape in central Paris. The cooking stays rooted in classic bourgeois French tradition while adding a contemporary touch, balancing familiarity with creativity. This is a good choice for diners who appreciate polished surroundings, heritage, and carefully composed cuisine in a historic setting.

name: Armani Ristorante
description: Set on the first floor of the Armani store in Saint-Germain-des-Prés, this Michelin-starred restaurant pairs a polished, design-led setting with contemporary Italian cooking. The room is understated and elegant, with beige tones, banquettes, glossy surfaces and soft lighting. In the kitchen, high-quality ingredients are handled with care to produce dishes that feel fresh, flavorful and precise. It should appeal to diners looking for a refined Italian meal in a stylish Paris address, especially those who appreciate a calm, modern atmosphere as much as the cooking.

name: Astrance
description: Astrance offers inventive French cuisine in a sleek, modern dining room in Paris’s 16th arrondissement. The kitchen shows a strong focus on produce, with dishes that often lean toward Asian and plant-based influences, while a signature mushroom millefeuille with foie gras remains on the menu. The setting balances contemporary style with a nod to its storied location, and the service is designed to make guests feel at ease. It should appeal to diners looking for creative cooking, a polished atmosphere, and a thoughtful wine selection.

name: AT
description: AT is a discreet, one-star contemporary restaurant near the Seine in Paris’s 5th arrondissement, with a minimalist setting that matches its understated approach. Chef Atsushi Tanaka focuses on fresh ingredients, precision, and imaginative cooking, shaping a single set menu that changes regularly. The experience is designed to keep diners surprised, with dishes that emphasize creativity as much as technique. A vaulted cellar below houses a wine bar and takeaway offerings. It should appeal to guests looking for a refined, quietly confident meal in an intimate setting.

name: Auguste
description: Auguste is a compact, one-star French restaurant in Paris 7e with a calm, chic interior and a refined contemporary feel. The cooking is modern and technically precise, built around market-led ingredients and a thoughtful approach to sourcing, including a focus on non-endangered fish. Dishes may feature combinations such as scallops with oyster mushrooms, black truffle, or sweetbreads with seasonal accents. A carefully chosen wine list, including wines by the glass served from magnums, adds to the appeal. It should suit diners looking for polished, inventive French cuisine in an intimate setting.

name: Automne
description: Automne is a Michelin-starred contemporary restaurant in Paris 11e where Japanese chef Nobuyuki Akishige presents refined seasonal cooking with a French sensibility. The kitchen focuses on fresh, top-quality ingredients transformed on site, with dishes that emphasize clarity and balance. A simple bistro-style interior keeps the mood relaxed and unpretentious, letting the food take center stage. Expect a concise, modern style rather than heavy elaboration, with thoughtful wine choices that lean toward natural wines. It should appeal to diners looking for precise, ingredient-led cuisine in a casual setting.

name: Baieta
description: Baieta is a Michelin-starred Paris dining room devoted to Mediterranean flavors, with a clear nod to Nice and the French Riviera. The cooking is colorful and fragrant, moving from bouillabaisse and aioli to socca, pissaladière, and other dishes shaped by a charcoal-fired oven. The menu also shows a creative, contemporary touch, pairing premium produce with thoughtful combinations and a surprise format. It should appeal to diners who enjoy refined regional cooking with personality, especially those drawn to seafood, Provençal accents, and a lively, fine-dining experience.

name: Bellefeuille
address: Saint James Paris, 5 place du Chancelier-Adenauer, 75116 Paris, France
description: Bellefeuille offers refined French dining in a secluded Paris mansion that feels like a small château, set amid greenery in the 16th arrondissement. The kitchen places vegetables and seafood at the center of the menu, drawing on produce from the property’s own garden, which supplies a wide range of fruit, herbs, and vegetables. Service is polished and informed, and the wine list is thoughtfully chosen. With its elegant setting and sustainability-minded approach, it will appeal to diners seeking a distinctive, quietly exclusive Michelin-starred experience in Paris.

name: Blanc
description: Blanc offers contemporary cooking in a refined, minimalist setting, with a circular dining room framed by interwoven wooden slats. Chef Shinichi Sato builds the menu around top-quality produce, using precise technique and exacting attention to detail. Dishes may feature ingredients such as Roscoff onions, Bellota chorizo, or Galician beef cooked over embers. The atmosphere is plush but restrained, with a hushed bar and a strong wine and spirits selection, including a Burgundy-focused list. It should appeal to diners who value technical finesse, carefully sourced ingredients, and a polished, quietly luxurious experience.

name: Chakaiseki Akiyoshi
description: Chakaiseki Akiyoshi offers a rare Parisian take on traditional Japanese chakaiseki, the meal associated with the tea ceremony. Set behind a discreet wooden façade, the intimate room seats just 16 and is designed to evoke an authentic tea house. The cooking is ingredient-led and focused on freshness, with dishes prepared in view of guests and presented with careful attention to colour, balance and tableware. This is a refined, contemplative experience for diners drawn to Japanese culinary tradition, visual artistry and a quietly exclusive atmosphere.

name: Comice
description: Comice is a Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris that blends Gallic tradition with a contemporary touch. The kitchen focuses on seasonal ingredients, shaping a single set menu around what is freshest at the time. Inside, the mood is polished and modern, with vivid blue walls and contemporary artwork giving the room a distinctive character. Service is described as warm and attentive, adding to the restaurant’s welcoming feel. It should appeal to diners who enjoy refined French cooking, thoughtful seasonal menus, and a stylish but relaxed setting.

name: Contraste
description: Contraste offers refined French dining in Paris’s 8th arrondissement, pairing the elegance of its setting with more contemporary design touches. The kitchen focuses on meticulous, up-to-date cooking built around seasonal produce, with flavours presented through several tasting menus. An extensive wine list adds to the experience, including notable champagne selections. With its polished atmosphere and careful attention to detail, it should appeal to diners looking for a modern, ingredient-led meal in a smart, star-level setting.

name: Datil
description: Datil is a Michelin-starred Paris restaurant led by chef Manon Fleury, whose cooking centers on sustainable fine dining and careful sourcing. The menu is largely plant-based, with fruits and vegetables sharing the spotlight and animal protein used sparingly. Her refined approach highlights the aromatic depth of grains and the sweet-sour balance of fruit, creating dishes that feel precise and thoughtful rather than heavy. The room is understated and contemporary, with waxed concrete floors, velvet banquettes, and light oak chairs. It should appeal to diners looking for a polished, ingredient-driven meal with a strong environmental ethos.

name: David Toutain
description: In a modern loft-style setting on a quiet Paris 7 street, this two-star restaurant presents an ambitious contemporary tasting experience built around surprise menus. Chef David Toutain’s cooking is marked by technical precision, clear flavors, and a strong sense of experimentation, reflecting a personal, modern style. The desserts are a notable highlight, and the wine list includes several well-priced bottles. It should appeal to diners who enjoy inventive cuisine, carefully composed dishes, and a polished but understated atmosphere.

name: Divellec
description: Divellec is a one-star Paris seafood restaurant with a distinctly maritime identity. Its menu focuses on top-quality fish and shellfish, with dishes that follow the seasons and highlight classic preparations such as sole meunière, cod aïoli with vegetables, and grilled langoustines. The cooking is traditional and clear in style, appealing to diners who appreciate straightforward, well-executed seafood. Set in a stylish interior, it suits guests looking for a refined yet unfussy meal centered on the sea.

name: Don Juan II
description: Don Juan II offers a Michelin-starred contemporary dining cruise on the Seine, aboard a lavish Art Deco yacht moored on the Right Bank opposite the Eiffel Tower. The setting is refined, with wainscoting and thick carpets creating a gracious, intimate atmosphere. At the helm is Frédéric Anton, whose cooking includes signature dishes such as curry-scented crab, langoustine ravioli, and warm chocolate soufflé. It should appeal to diners seeking a special-occasion meal that combines polished service, classic luxury, and memorable Paris views.

name: Episodes
description: Episodes is a Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris’s Monceau district, set in a smart, intimate room finished with marble, wood, and stone. The cooking is presented through a modern tasting menu, with an emphasis on outstanding produce, precise technique, and refined, delicate flavors. The atmosphere feels urban and chic, yet also comfortable and sincere. Guests can expect a style that balances classic French foundations with contemporary expression, making it well suited to diners who appreciate craftsmanship, elegant presentation, and a focused, small-scale dining experience.

name: ES
description: ES is a one-star French restaurant in Paris led by Japanese chef Takayuki Honjo, whose cooking reflects both French technique and a subtle Japanese sensibility. The dining room is pared back and serene, creating a calm setting where the meal feels almost ceremonial. The kitchen favors refined, carefully composed dishes with distinctive pairings such as foie gras with sea urchin or pigeon with cocoa. Guests who appreciate precise, understated fine dining and thoughtful flavor combinations are likely to enjoy its quiet, elegant style.

name: Espadon
address: Ritz Paris, 15 place Vendôme, 75001 Paris, France
description: Set inside the Ritz Paris on Place Vendôme, Espadon offers a refined, quietly luxurious setting that suits its one-star level of polish. Chef Eugénie Béziat shapes a fusion style that draws on African childhood memories and Mediterranean influences, creating dishes with smoky, tangy, and roasted accents. The cooking is inventive yet balanced, with a focus on clear flavors and elegant presentation. Standout plates include grilled oyster with paracress and brousse cheese, and grilled lobster with spinach, manioc, bissap, and crushed raspberries. It should appeal to diners looking for a sophisticated, contemporary tasting experience in a historic Parisian hotel.

name: FIEF
address: 44 rue de la Folie-Méricourt, 75011 Paris, France
description: FIEF is a contemporary Paris restaurant built around a clear French-first philosophy, using only 100% French produce in inventive, highly personal dishes. The cooking blends familiar regional ingredients with unexpected accents, creating bold flavors and sauces with real character. Its counter seating adds to the experience, offering a close view of the kitchen and a chance to follow the team’s explanations as each plate is assembled. With its focused, modern approach and one-star recognition, it should appeal to diners who enjoy thoughtful, ingredient-led cuisine and an interactive dining atmosphere.

name: Fleur de Pavé
description: Fleur de Pavé is a Michelin-starred contemporary restaurant in Paris where chef Sylvain Sendra presents modern dishes inspired by flavors from around the world. The cooking emphasizes clear, well-judged taste over showy technique, with carefully sourced ingredients and seasonal vegetables playing a notable role. Plating is polished yet unfussy, and the overall approach feels current without losing focus. The wine list is a particular strength for a restaurant of this size, with some rare bottles to explore. It should appeal to diners looking for thoughtful, ingredient-led fine dining in a relaxed, up-to-date setting.

name: Frenchie
description: Frenchie is a compact, Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris’s Le Sentier district, where exposed brick, beams, and stonework create a warm, intimate setting. Chef Grégory Marchand’s cooking reflects international influences while staying grounded in careful sourcing and seasonal produce. The result is a polished, contemporary style that feels both precise and approachable. With its small dining room and strong reputation, it should appeal to diners looking for a refined meal in a lively, tightly run space with a modern outlook on French cuisine.

name: Frédéric Simonin
description: Frédéric Simonin offers refined French cooking in a setting designed to feel like a Parisian apartment, with hardwood floors, white walls, and bevelled mirrors. The cuisine is precise, delicate, and well balanced, with particular attention to sauces and reductions. Guests can choose from multi-course tasting menus, a strong lunch option, and a thoughtful selection of wines by the glass. With its calm, polished atmosphere and technically assured cooking, it should appeal to diners looking for a classic yet personal Michelin-starred experience in Paris.

name: Galanga
address: Hôtel Monsieur George, 17 rue Washington, 75008 Paris, France
description: Set inside an Art Deco-inspired boutique hotel near the Champs-Élysées, Galanga offers a polished but relaxed fine-dining setting. The contemporary menu leans Mediterranean, with vegetables taking a leading role and spices used with restraint and purpose. Dishes are crafted with precision, balancing vivid flavors, rich sauces, and careful seasoning; desserts are equally refined. The restaurant should appeal to diners looking for a modern, ingredient-led meal in an elegant room, with attentive service and a clear emphasis on finesse rather than formality.

name: Gaya
description: Gaya is a Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris with the feel of a chic, uncluttered brasserie. Its bright blue façade and elegant setting create a polished but comfortable atmosphere that suits relaxed fine dining. The cooking leans toward a precise, delicate style with a clear seafood focus, from shellfish and fish dishes to refined desserts. It should appeal to diners looking for a stylish, food-driven meal in a contemporary Paris setting, especially those who enjoy modern French cuisine with a maritime edge.

name: Geoélia
address: 125 rue de la Tour, 75116 Paris, France
description: Geoélia is a Michelin-starred modern restaurant in Paris’s 16th arrondissement, set in an elegant street-corner space with bright contemporary lines and striking Murano chandeliers. Chef Camille Saint-M’Leux builds a seasonal menu shaped by the tides, with a strong emphasis on fish, seafood and vegetables, presented with precise, graphic plating. Dishes may include crab, line-caught pollock or blue lobster, while meat options are also available. The atmosphere feels polished and refined, making it a good fit for diners who appreciate seasonal cooking, careful presentation and a menu that balances marine-focused cuisine with broader choice.

name: Granite
description: Near the Louvre, Granite offers refined French cooking in a setting of crisp, contemporary elegance. The kitchen focuses on impeccable ingredients and precise, delicate execution, with subtle Japanese accents woven into the menu. Dishes are presented with care, and the pastry work is especially well integrated into the overall progression. The room combines a relaxed, modern energy with polished service, creating a calm but stylish dining experience. It should appeal to diners looking for a thoughtful, inventive meal that balances technical finesse with a distinctive, understated personality.

name: Géosmine
address: 71 rue de la Folie-Méricourt, 75011 Paris, France
description: Géosmine is a contemporary Michelin-starred restaurant in Paris’s Oberkampf area, set in a two-storey townhouse with a distinctly eastern-Paris feel. The cooking is led by a young chef from Sarthe who builds a seasonal tasting menu around pared-back, carefully judged dishes rather than heavy complexity. A thoughtful wine list adds appeal, with natural and biodynamic selections alongside notable Italian, Spanish, and Austrian bottles. This is a good fit for diners who appreciate restrained, modern cuisine in a characterful neighborhood setting, especially those interested in wine as part of the experience.

name: Hakuba
address: Cheval Blanc Paris, 8 quai du Louvre, 75001 Paris, France
description: Hakuba is a refined Japanese restaurant in Paris that pairs precise sushi with a broader menu shaped by exceptional sauces, broths, and delicate seasonal accents. The dining room emphasizes elegance and clarity, with service and decor designed to match the kitchen’s polished style. Sushi is prepared in full view, adding a sense of craft and theater, while dessert receives unusual attention for a Japanese setting. This is a strong choice for diners seeking a highly composed, luxury experience where technique, purity of ingredients, and balance are central.

name: Hanada
description: Hanada is an intimate sushi counter on Paris’s Left Bank, offering a focused omakase experience built around carefully selected fish and precise preparation. The small hinoki counter places guests close to the chef and his team, creating a quiet, restrained atmosphere that emphasizes craft over spectacle. Nigiri and maki are presented on refined tableware, with a short wine list and a thoughtful sake selection completing the meal. This is a strong choice for diners who value meticulous technique, elegant simplicity, and a calm, highly personal Japanese dining experience.

name: Héritages
description: Héritages offers an intimate Michelin-starred experience in Paris’s 8th arrondissement, with just a handful of tables and a calm, understated setting. The cooking is modern French, rooted in tradition but open in inspiration, with a strong emphasis on first-class produce. Expect refined dishes shaped by technically precise, generously built sauces and a polished, seamless pace of service. Its restrained, contemporary atmosphere suits diners looking for a quiet, exclusive meal where the kitchen’s craft and attention to detail take center stage.

name: Il Carpaccio
address: Le Royal Monceau, 37 avenue Hoche, 75008 Paris, France
description: Il Carpaccio offers refined Italian cooking in an elegant setting at the Royal Monceau in Paris. The dining room feels like a spring-toned winter garden, with an artistic, decorative approach that adds to the sense of occasion. The kitchen focuses on precise, flavourful dishes that let carefully chosen ingredients speak for themselves, without unnecessary embellishment. A thoughtful wine list leans toward Piedmont and Tuscany. This is a strong choice for diners seeking polished Italian cuisine in a stylish, serene atmosphere, especially those who appreciate ingredient-led cooking and classic regional wine pairings.

name: Imperial Treasure
description: Imperial Treasure offers refined Chinese fine dining in Paris, with a focus on traditional, authentic dishes and a sense of occasion. The menu highlights classic preparations such as tableside-carved Peking duck, dim sum, and chicken in Chinese yellow wine, all presented with care and lavish ingredients. Service is attentive and polished, with staff guiding diners through the experience. Near the Champs-Élysées, it suits guests looking for a formal meal that emphasizes heritage, sharing, and well-executed regional specialties in an elegant setting.

name: Irwin
description: Irwin is a Michelin-starred modern restaurant in Paris 8e offering a seasonal surprise menu built around market-fresh ingredients. The cooking leans refined and technically precise, with dishes that balance creativity and classic French references. Its small dining room and table d’hôte facing the open kitchen create an intimate, focused atmosphere. Backed by a strong team in the kitchen and pastry section, the restaurant suits diners looking for a polished, contemporary tasting experience with flair, detail, and a sense of occasion.

name: Jacques Faussat
description: Jacques Faussat offers French gastronomic cooking in Paris’s 17th arrondissement, shaped by a clear preference for simplicity and well-chosen produce. The kitchen focuses on flavours and aromas, with dishes built to feel generous and approachable rather than overly elaborate. Its Michelin-starred status and relatively accessible pricing make it appealing to diners seeking refined cooking without formality. This is a good fit for guests who appreciate classic French technique, ingredient-led plates, and a restaurant that balances ambition with ease.

name: Jean Imbert
description: Set within an iconic luxury hotel on Avenue Montaigne, this one-star restaurant offers a polished take on French cuisine that looks to the country’s culinary heritage while adding a contemporary touch. The menu features classic preparations such as Dieppe-style seabass, Albufera poultry, venison à la royale, and an elaborate grand dessert. The setting is grand and theatrical, with gilt detailing and a marble table d’hôte in the Régence hall. Service is attentive and well-drilled, making it a strong choice for diners seeking an elegant, indulgent Paris experience.

name: Jin
address: 6 rue de la Sourdière, 75001 Paris, France
description: Jin is an intimate Japanese restaurant in Paris, set between the Louvre and Place Vendôme, with seating for only a handful of guests at a handsome wooden counter. The experience centers on an omakase menu, where skilled chefs prepare sushi and sashimi with precise knife work and careful attention to timing. Top-quality fish, including produce from Brittany and Spain, is matured for peak flavor. The atmosphere is discreet and polished, with service to match. It will appeal to diners seeking a refined, chef-led sushi experience and a strong sake selection in an exclusive setting.

name: Kei
description: Kei is a three-star French restaurant in Paris 1er led by chef Kei Kobayashi, whose cooking blends French technique with a Japanese sensibility. The cuisine is precise and highly refined, with small, impeccably composed dishes that highlight top-quality ingredients through clear, elegant flavors. Expect meticulous plating and a menu that feels both technically assured and easy to read. It suits diners looking for a polished gastronomic experience where craftsmanship, balance, and ingredient quality take center stage.

name: L'Abysse au Pavillon Ledoyen
description: L'Abysse au Pavillon Ledoyen offers a refined Japanese dining experience shaped around sushi and exceptional ingredients, including carefully sourced fish. The room is pared back and elegant, with contemporary art adding a modern touch and a small counter that places guests close to the action. Service is polished, and the drinks list stands out for its selection of rare sakes alongside fine wines. This is a strong choice for diners seeking a precise, high-end meal in a calm, intimate setting, especially those interested in sushi craftsmanship and a contemporary luxury atmosphere.

name: L'Ambroisie
description: Set on the Place des Vosges, L’Ambroisie offers classic French gastronomy in a refined, timeless setting. Antique mirrors, marble floors, a large tapestry and contemporary wall panels create an elegant, slightly dramatic atmosphere. The kitchen remains rooted in exacting technique and exceptional ingredients, with dishes that reflect a traditional French base while allowing a subtle Japanese influence under chef Shintaro Awa. It will appeal to diners seeking a formal, highly polished experience and a cuisine that values precision, restraint and craftsmanship.

name: L'Archeste
address: 79 rue de la Tour, 75016 Paris, France
description: L'Archeste is a one-star French restaurant in Paris 16e with a sleek, light-filled dining room and a polished, contemporary feel. The cooking is modern and precise, with a strong focus on premium ingredients and seasonal produce. Menus change daily, reflecting the chef’s inspiration rather than a fixed format, which adds a sense of spontaneity to the experience. This is a good choice for diners who appreciate refined, ingredient-led French cuisine in a stylish setting and enjoy tasting menus that evolve with the seasons.

name: L'Arpège
description: L'Arpège is a three-star French restaurant in Paris known for Alain Passard’s vegetable-led cooking, now entirely free of animal protein. The menu reflects his close connection to his kitchen gardens, with fruit, flowers, and produce at the center of inventive dishes such as smoked potatoes with wine sauce. The dining room adds to the experience with a bucolic fresco and a calm, refined atmosphere. It will appeal to diners interested in highly original French cuisine, especially those drawn to seasonal vegetables and a distinctive chef-gardener philosophy.

name: L'Arôme
description: L’Arôme is a Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris 8e that blends tradition with a modern, generous touch. Under Thomas Boullault, the kitchen focuses on seasonal produce and polished, straightforward dishes, paired with carefully crafted sauces and refined ingredients. The dining room is intimate and elegant, with a warm, feel-good atmosphere that suits both special occasions and relaxed fine dining. Guests can expect a confident, ingredient-led menu and attentive hospitality, making it a strong choice for diners who appreciate classic French cooking presented with contemporary clarity.

name: L'Atelier de Joël Robuchon - Étoile
description: L'Atelier de Joël Robuchon - Étoile offers a contemporary take on French fine dining just off the Champs-Élysées, near the Arc de Triomphe. Guests sit on high stools at a long counter facing the kitchen, creating a calm, focused atmosphere. The menu includes both tasting-style portions and fuller plates, with a vegetarian option available. Its appeal lies in precise, modern cooking and a polished setting that suits diners who enjoy watching the kitchen in action. Wine lovers may also appreciate the by-the-glass selection served from magnums.

name: L'Oiseau Blanc
address: The Peninsula Paris, 19 avenue Kléber, 75116 Paris, France
description: L'Oiseau Blanc offers contemporary French fine dining in a striking rooftop setting above The Peninsula Paris. Encased in glass and perched over the city, it creates the feeling of dining in flight, with terrace views toward the Eiffel Tower and Sacré Cœur. The kitchen emphasizes precise cooking, well-judged sauces and appealing presentation, while the desserts are a particular strength. Its elegant, luxury-hotel atmosphere and panoramic outlook make it well suited to diners seeking a refined Paris experience with a memorable sense of place.

name: L'Orangerie
address: Four Seasons George V, 31 avenue George-V, 75008 Paris, France
description: Set in a glazed courtyard extension at the George V, L'Orangerie pairs a refined, contemporary setting with a cuisine centered on vegetables, dairy, and seafood, with meat notably absent. The cooking is precise and balanced, with light sauces that weave together maritime and vegetal notes to add subtle surprise. Elegant, discreet service and a striking hanging porcelain sculpture contribute to the polished atmosphere. This is a strong choice for diners who appreciate technically exact cooking, a lighter style of fine dining, and a serene luxury hotel setting in Paris.

name: La Grande Cascade
address: Allée de Longchamp, Bois de Boulogne, 75016 Paris, France
description: Set in the heart of the Bois de Boulogne, this one-star French restaurant pairs classic Parisian elegance with a peaceful, almost country-like setting. Its rotunda dining room, glazed roof and terrace evoke Empire, Belle Époque and Art Nouveau charm, creating a refined backdrop for a meal. The kitchen brings a modern touch to the historic atmosphere, with polished cooking and carefully crafted desserts. It suits diners looking for a special occasion address, whether for a romantic dinner or a leisurely escape from the city’s bustle.

name: La Scène
description: La Scène offers refined French cuisine with a generous, story-driven approach that aims to make haute cuisine feel less formal. Menus are built as a series of “acts,” with dishes that may look simple but are carefully composed, often anchored by rich sauces and seasonal ingredients. The atmosphere is relaxed and open, with warm, attentive service and a dining room that encourages guests to enjoy the moment. A strong choice for diners who appreciate polished cooking, thoughtful details, and a more approachable take on fine dining in Paris.

name: La Scène Thélème
description: La Scène Thélème is a discreet Michelin-starred address in Paris 17e where French and Japanese influences are blended with care. The cooking is described as refined and delicate, with carefully chosen ingredients and clear Japanese accents, while the setting adds a cultured, contemporary feel. Its appeal lies in precise, personality-driven dishes, attentive wine guidance, and a lunch offering noted for being relatively reasonable. This is a good fit for diners who enjoy elegant, understated fine dining with a cross-cultural style and a polished but unshowy atmosphere.

name: Lapérouse
description: Lapérouse is a storied Paris 6th arrondissement restaurant known for its intimate, softly lit salons and a celebratory atmosphere. It presents French gastronomy in a setting designed for memorable dinners, combining a sense of tradition with an elegant, discreet mood. The house suits guests looking for a classic Parisian experience with a touch of occasion, whether for a romantic evening, a special celebration, or a refined meal in a historic address by the Seine.

name: Lasserre
description: Lasserre is a classic French fine-dining address in Paris 8e, set in a Directoire-style mansion just off the Champs-Élysées. Its lavish dining room, with chandeliers, silverware, orchids and a retractable roof, creates a refined, theatrical setting that feels both historic and romantic. The kitchen updates its culinary heritage with a personal touch, including Mediterranean accents, while staying rooted in French gastronomy. This is a strong choice for diners seeking an elegant, special-occasion meal in a memorable setting, especially those who value tradition, atmosphere and polished service.

name: Le Baudelaire
address: Le Burgundy, 6-8 rue Duphot, 75001 Paris, France
description: Le Baudelaire offers a polished take on modern French dining inside a stylish luxury hotel in central Paris. The room is arranged around an inner courtyard and winter garden, with floor-to-ceiling windows, black lacquered tables, comfortable armchairs and a calm, contemporary feel. The cooking is described as well balanced and ingredient-led, with a particular emphasis on carefully sourced vegetables. It should appeal to diners looking for a refined, tranquil setting and a cuisine that leans toward freshness and restraint rather than excess.

name: Le Cinq
address: Four Seasons Hotel George V, 31 avenue George-V, 75008 Paris, France
description: Set inside one of Paris’s grand palace hotels, this three-star dining room pairs formal elegance with a bright, refined atmosphere. The cuisine is classic French at its most polished, with a strong emphasis on exceptional produce, precise technique, and delicate flavors. Brittany appears throughout the menu, especially in seafood dishes and thoughtful nods to regional ingredients. Desserts lean toward natural sweetness, with fruit and honey taking center stage. Guests who appreciate luxurious surroundings, meticulous service, and highly crafted fine dining will feel at home here.

name: Le Clarence
description: Le Clarence offers contemporary fine dining in a lavish 19th-century mansion near the Champs-Élysées. Under chef Andrea Capasso, the kitchen favors a disciplined yet expressive style, with bold flavors, surf-and-turf combinations, and occasional Italian accents woven into carefully focused menus. The setting is elegant and richly appointed, creating a distinctly polished Parisian atmosphere. A serious wine list adds to the appeal, making it a strong choice for diners who value refined cooking, a grand setting, and a memorable special-occasion experience.

name: Le Faham
description: Le Faham offers a refined take on Réunionese cuisine in a sleek, elegant setting in Paris’s 17th arrondissement. The cooking is marked by vivid flavors, delicate seasoning and a subtle spicy edge, with an exotic touch that gives the menu its identity. Dishes may weave together influences and ingredients in a polished, contemporary style, while the pastry work adds a memorable finish. This is a good choice for diners seeking a distinctive gastronomic experience that feels both composed and expressive, especially those interested in modern cuisine with island inspiration.

name: Le Gabriel
address: La Réserve Paris, 42 avenue Gabriel, 75008 Paris, France
description: Le Gabriel offers refined French dining inside La Réserve Paris, a grand 19th-century mansion near the Champs-Élysées. The setting is richly appointed, with gilded leather, parquet floors, and a polished, palace-like atmosphere. Chef Jérôme Banctel presents two tasting menus that reflect both Breton roots and wider travels, alongside seasonal game and a lunchtime à la carte selection. The cooking is known for its range of flavors and textures, from bright and sweet-sour notes to spicier, brinier accents. It should appeal to diners seeking an elegant, highly composed tasting experience in a luxurious setting.

name: Le George
address: Four Seasons George V, 31 avenue George-V, 75008 Paris, France
description: Le George offers Italian-inspired cooking in a polished setting inside the Four Seasons George V. The dining room pairs white decor, floral accents, and a striking chandelier with a relaxed, elegant feel, while the conservatory adds a bright courtyard view. The cuisine is light and Mediterranean-leaning, with careful attention to flavor and cooking. Small portions and a refined presentation suit diners looking for a stylish, contemporary take on Italian food in a luxury hotel setting. It is a strong choice for guests who value atmosphere as much as the plate.

name: Le Grand Restaurant
description: Le Grand Restaurant offers a refined French tasting-menu experience in a compact, intimate dining room beneath a striking glass ceiling. The cuisine draws on classic French gastronomy while presenting it with a modern touch, with menus that travel through the regions of France and highlight the chef’s signature slow-cooked preparations. The setting feels focused and elegant rather than showy, and the cooking emphasizes clarity, depth of flavor, and technical confidence. It should appeal to diners seeking a polished, contemporary expression of French fine dining in a discreet Paris address.

name: Le Grand Véfour
description: Le Grand Véfour is a historic Paris address in the 1st arrondissement, set in the Péristyle de Joinville. With no source details on the current menu or service style, the restaurant can only be described conservatively as a classic dining destination in a central, elegant setting. It may appeal to guests seeking a notable Parisian location for a refined meal, especially those drawn to landmark restaurants with a sense of heritage and place.

name: Le Jules Verne
address: Tour Eiffel - 6 avenue Gustave-Eiffel, 75007 Paris, France
description: Perched on the second floor of the Eiffel Tower, Le Jules Verne pairs a dramatic Paris setting with polished French fine dining. The kitchen focuses on precise, delicately composed dishes built around top-quality produce, with an emphasis on balance and clean execution rather than excess. Expect a refined tasting experience, attentive service, and desserts that match the kitchen’s high standards. The panoramic views over Paris add to the sense of occasion, making this a strong choice for diners seeking a special-occasion meal where location, technique, and elegance all matter.

name: Le Pré Catelan
address: Route de Suresnes - bois de Boulogne, 75016 Paris, France
description: Set in a historic lodge in the Bois de Boulogne, Le Pré Catelan offers polished, contemporary French fine dining in a refined, design-led setting. Under Frédéric Anton, the kitchen focuses on top-quality ingredients and a tasting menu built with precision, restraint, and clear technical control. The atmosphere is elegant and composed, with green, white, and silver tones and a sense of occasion throughout. It will appeal to diners seeking a highly accomplished, classic-leaning experience at the top end of Paris dining, especially those who value finesse, balance, and meticulous execution.

name: Le Sergent Recruteur
description: Le Sergent Recruteur is a one-star contemporary restaurant on Île Saint-Louis, set in a historic former tavern that now feels refined and quietly modern. The cooking is precise and classically grounded, with an emphasis on delicate flavors, careful technique, and well-judged sauces. Dishes are presented with coherence and a polished sense of balance, making the meal feel composed rather than showy. The dining room has a hushed, elegant atmosphere that blends contemporary design with old walls, appealing to guests who appreciate thoughtful fine dining in a distinctive Paris setting.

name: Le Tout-Paris
address: Cheval Blanc Paris, 8 quai du Louvre, 75001 Paris, France
description: Le Tout-Paris is a polished brasserie on the seventh floor of Cheval Blanc Paris, combining contemporary flair with classic French references. Its colourful interior and terrace overlooking the Seine and the Left Bank create a lively, elegant setting. The kitchen balances brasserie traditions with careful craftsmanship, offering dishes such as macaroni with foie gras, truffle and artichoke, or grilled sea bass with sauce vierge. Desserts are also a highlight. It should appeal to diners seeking a stylish, upscale Parisian experience with strong views and refined cooking.

name: Le Violon d'Ingres
description: Le Violon d’Ingres is a Michelin-starred French neo-brasserie in Paris 7e, near the Eiffel Tower, serving refined cuisine with a focus on France’s South-West. The kitchen is known for technical precision and high-quality ingredients, with signature dishes such as sweetbreads in vin jaune sauce and a classic millefeuille with light vanilla cream. The atmosphere is lively yet polished, with a strong sense of conviviality and a highly professional service team. It should appeal to diners seeking a stylish, traditional-leaning Parisian meal in an energetic setting.

name: Lucas Carton
address: 9 place de la Madeleine, 75008 Paris, France
description: Lucas Carton is a historic French fine-dining address in Paris’s 8th arrondissement, known for its ornate Art Nouveau setting with carved woodwork. Under chef Hugo Bourny, the cooking is personal and creative, with a notable vegetable emphasis and dishes shaped by aromatic, spicy, or acidic accents. The style aims for clear, direct flavors rather than excess, making the meal feel precise and lively. It should appeal to diners who value classic Parisian atmosphere alongside contemporary, ingredient-led cuisine, especially those drawn to a refined but expressive tasting experience.

name: Maison Dubois
description: Maison Dubois offers intimate fine dining in Paris with just a handful of tables and a discreet, house-like atmosphere. The cooking is French at heart, balancing classic foundations with a more inventive approach to flavor and presentation. Careful sourcing and precise technique are central, with dishes that rework familiar ideas in thoughtful ways. The setting and style suit diners looking for a quiet, personal meal where craftsmanship and product quality take priority. It should appeal especially to guests who enjoy refined, contemporary interpretations of classic cuisine.

name: Maison Rostang
description: Maison Rostang is a long-established Parisian address for classic French gastronomy, led with a contemporary touch. The cooking balances tradition and modernity through a concise à la carte selection and tasting menus, including a plant-based option. Signature dishes range from pike-perch quenelle with lobster cream to Bresse chicken with salsify and yellow wine jus, while desserts are given equal care. The recently refurbished dining room feels plush and polished, with a view into the kitchen. It should appeal to diners seeking refined, familiar French flavors in an elegant setting.

name: Maison Ruggieri
description: Maison Ruggieri offers a creative, refined dining experience in the Palais Royal, where Martino Ruggieri showcases an inventive Italian-leaning style. The menu is built around bold combinations, powerful flavors, and expressive sauces, with dishes that move confidently beyond tradition. Its elegant setting adds to the sense of occasion, making it well suited to diners seeking a distinctive Michelin-starred meal in central Paris. Those who enjoy imaginative cooking and a polished atmosphere will find plenty to appreciate here.

name: Mallory Gabsi
description: Mallory Gabsi is a one-star French restaurant in Paris led by Brussels-born chef Mallory Gabsi, whose cooking is marked by energy, precision and a taste for bold contrasts. The menu balances acidic and bitter notes and includes inventive dishes that nod to his Belgian roots, alongside playful, unexpected flavor pairings. The dining room is plush and subdued, with Art Deco touches and a refined, old-world feel. It should appeal to diners who enjoy contemporary, creative cuisine in an elegant setting, especially those drawn to adventurous tasting menus and distinctive flavor combinations.

name: Marsan
description: Marsan is a refined French restaurant in Paris that pairs an elegant dining room with cooking shaped by Hélène Darroze’s roots in southwest France. The menu highlights carefully sourced ingredients and a distinctive sense of creativity, with dishes that balance tradition and modern flair. Expect polished presentation and a confident, ingredient-led style, from rich foie gras to lobster and classic desserts with a contemporary touch. It should appeal to diners looking for a sophisticated, high-level French experience with clear personality and strong culinary craftsmanship.

name: Mavrommatis
description: Mavrommatis offers refined Greek cuisine in Paris, shaped by classic French technique and the flavors of the Hellenic tradition. The cooking highlights seasonal ingredients and dishes such as grilled octopus, phyllo, lamb, and dolmades, presented with care and precision. The restaurant is set in an elegant, evocative dining room that suits a polished meal. It will appeal to diners looking for a thoughtful, contemporary expression of Greek cooking with a Michelin-starred level of finesse and a strong sense of place.

name: Monsieur Dior
description: Set on the first floor of Dior’s historic Avenue Montaigne boutique, this one-star restaurant pairs a bright, elegant dining room with a distinctly fashion-led identity. The modern menu draws inspiration from haute couture, with refined dishes such as sea bass, a signature egg with ham and caviar, poached sole with beurre blanc and shellfish, and poularde with black truffle macaroni. Dior tableware and the setting add to the sense of occasion. It will appeal to diners seeking polished, luxurious surroundings and carefully composed cuisine in a highly exclusive address.

name: MoSuke
description: MoSuke is a one-star Paris restaurant offering an original fusion of Malian and Senegalese roots, Japanese influences, and French culinary technique. The cooking is distinctive and accomplished, with a clear emphasis on layered flavors and inventive combinations. Dishes may include flame-torched langoustines, croaker with fermented cabbage and citrus, or Aubrac beef with mafé sauce and togarashi. The setting suggests a thoughtful, culturally rooted experience that feels both modern and personal. It should appeal to diners looking for creative cuisine that pushes beyond familiar boundaries.

name: Nakatani
description: Nakatani is an intimate, one-star Paris restaurant where Japanese chef Shinsuke Nakatani presents a seasonal French tasting menu shaped by precise seasoning, careful presentation, and balanced textures. The dining room is calm and elegant, with soothing colors and natural materials that create a restful atmosphere. Service is discreet and professional, and the small number of seats makes the experience feel exclusive. With a single menu that changes regularly, it will appeal to diners who enjoy refined French cooking, seasonal ingredients, and a quietly polished setting.

name: Neige d'Été
description: Neige d’Été is a one-star French restaurant in Paris led by Japanese chef Hideki Nishi, whose training in classic Parisian kitchens informs a style that blends Japanese precision with French technique. The cooking is subtle and carefully composed, with a strong emphasis on fish and vegetables from Brittany, plus occasional barbecued accents. The atmosphere suggested by the cuisine is refined and restrained, with an eye for contrast and balance. It should appeal to diners who enjoy elegant, understated tasting menus and thoughtful, technically precise cooking with a distinctly cross-cultural character.

name: NESO
description: NESO offers a modern French tasting experience shaped by Guillaume Sanchez’s bold, flame-led cooking. The kitchen pairs pure French produce with vivid accents such as fermentation, spice, herbs and precise technical touches, creating dishes with striking contrasts in flavor and texture. The setting is stylish and arty, with a dark palette and raw materials that suit the restaurant’s energetic personality. Diners who enjoy inventive, expressive cuisine and an immersive counter seat will find plenty to engage them here.

name: Nhome
description: Nhome offers a creative contemporary tasting menu shaped by Scandinavian ideas and layered with Japanese and Middle Eastern influences. Set in an atmospheric vaulted cellar opposite the Palais Royal, the room centers on a single large communal table, giving the experience a lively, social feel. The kitchen uses a range of precise techniques, including fermentation, brining, low-temperature cooking and binchotan charcoal, to build distinctive dishes with strong technical control. With its attentive service and inventive approach, it should appeal to diners looking for an original, modern fine-dining experience in central Paris.

name: Nomicos
description: Nomicos is a one-star Paris restaurant led by Jean-Louis Nomicos, whose cooking draws on Mediterranean roots and a respect for classical technique. The menu emphasizes carefully chosen ingredients and dishes shaped by flavor and texture, with Mediterranean influences highlighted throughout. Signature preparations include macaroni with black truffles and duck foie gras. Set in a contemporary dining room, the atmosphere offers a modern backdrop to a style that feels polished and tradition-minded. It should appeal to diners who appreciate refined, ingredient-led cuisine with a distinctly Mediterranean sensibility.

name: Omar Dhiab
description: Near Place des Victoires, Omar Dhiab offers a pared-back, contemporary setting with an open kitchen and a polished marble counter. The cooking is French at its core, but it leans inventive, with a strong focus on expressive ingredients, precise sauces and seasonings, and occasional Egyptian accents. Dishes may range from sea urchin served simply to game paired with herbal or smoky notes, while desserts stay light and seasonal. With its youthful, energetic service, the restaurant should appeal to diners who enjoy refined, creative cuisine in a sleek, intimate atmosphere.

name: Onor
description: Onor is a contemporary, Michelin-starred brasserie in Paris with a refined maritime feel and a luxurious yet intimate setting. The cooking balances modern technique with a strong respect for produce, producers, and seasonal rhythms, while weaving in subtle Asian accents. Signature dishes may include soy risotto and seasonal seafood preparations such as scallops or seabream. With its polished atmosphere and precise, delicate style, it should appeal to diners looking for thoughtful, modern cuisine in an elegant brasserie setting.

name: Origines
description: Origines is a contemporary Michelin-starred restaurant in Paris 8e where chef Julien Boscus draws on a strong classical foundation to create bold, precise modern cooking. The à la carte menu highlights seasonal produce in carefully composed dishes, with a focus on flavour and texture. Expect polished execution, from crisp starters to refined meat and game preparations, alongside an impressive French wine selection. The atmosphere is professional yet welcoming, making it a good choice for diners who appreciate thoughtful gastronomy, a genuine menu choice, and a style that balances tradition with a modern edge.

name: Oxte
description: Oxte is a small, stylish restaurant in Paris’s Étoile area serving contemporary Mexican-influenced cuisine with a French touch. The cooking is seasonal and expressive, built around bold condiments, herbs and spices, with dishes that balance precision and vibrancy. Expect a menu that changes regularly and often nods to Mexico through thoughtful combinations and punchy seasoning. The atmosphere is intimate and fashionable, suited to diners looking for a refined, modern tasting experience. It should appeal to guests who enjoy creative cross-cultural cooking and well-judged, flavor-forward plates.

name: Pages
description: Pages offers a contemporary, Japanese-influenced take on French cuisine, built around carefully sourced produce from Normandy, Brittany, and beyond. The dining room is pared back and bright, with bare white walls that create a calm, understated setting. Menus are presented as a surprise sequence, with dishes shaped by precise cooking, vivid flavors, and occasional binchotan grilling for a subtle smoky edge. The restaurant’s strengths lie in the quality of ingredients, technical control, and polished service. It should appeal to diners who enjoy modern, refined tasting menus with a creative cross-cultural perspective.

name: Pantagruel
description: Pantagruel is a one-star Paris restaurant serving modern French cuisine with a playful, precise touch. Near the Palais Royal, it pairs a colourful, eclectic dining room with a youthful, polished service team. The cooking leans inventive and textural, with dishes built around crisp execution and multiple preparations of the same ingredient. Signature ideas include lobster toastie, textured egg and leek, and Breton lobster with mousseline and bisque. It should appeal to diners looking for contemporary gastronomy in a lively, characterful setting.

name: Pavyllon
description: Pavyllon offers a contemporary take on classic French foundations, shaped by Yannick Alléno’s modern vision. The restaurant is known for its convivial counter seating facing the open kitchen, creating a lively, interactive experience. Dishes are described as refined and delicate, with international accents adding interest without overpowering the core style. The setting is chic yet relaxed, with a smart dining room and a terrace surrounded by greenery. It should appeal to diners looking for polished cooking in an elegant but approachable atmosphere.

name: Pertinence
description: Pertinence is a one-star Paris restaurant near the Eiffel Tower, where French cuisine is reworked with Japanese techniques and a subtle fusion sensibility. The room is intimate and elegant, with a minimalist design of light wood and clean-lined seating that creates a calm, cosy atmosphere. In the kitchen, market-fresh ingredients are turned into dishes with clear, confident flavors and a modern take on French tradition. It should appeal to diners looking for refined cooking in a restrained setting, especially those interested in thoughtful cross-cultural cuisine.

name: Pierre Gagnaire
description: Pierre Gagnaire offers modern French haute cuisine in a refined Paris setting. The dining room is understated and subtly sophisticated, with a striking contemporary artwork that sets an artistic tone. The cooking is known for its adventurous, ever-evolving style, with poetic dishes presented in inventive small portions rather than a fixed signature plate. Service is described as well matched to the room’s elegance. This is a destination for diners who appreciate creative, ambitious gastronomy and a restaurant that treats each course as part of a constantly renewed experience.

name: Pilgrim
description: Pilgrim is a Michelin-starred modern restaurant in Paris 15e led by chef Masaki Nagao. The cooking is crisp, seasonal, and subtly inventive, with a Gallic base shaped by Japanese precision. Shellfish plays a recurring role, appearing raw, cooked, or in broth, alongside dishes that can move from vegetable compositions to scallops with pear and smoked ricotta. The atmosphere suggests a refined gastronomic experience with an exploratory spirit. It should appeal to diners who enjoy contemporary French cuisine with delicate contrasts and a concise lunch option.

name: Plénitude
address: Cheval Blanc Paris, 8 quai du Louvre, 75001 Paris, France
description: Plénitude is a luxurious three-star dining room inside Cheval Blanc Paris, set in the restored Samaritaine building in central Paris. The cooking takes a contemporary approach to classical French cuisine, drawing on Normandy, the Mediterranean and the Paris region. Its hallmark is meticulous attention to ingredients and especially to sauces, jus and dressings, which shape the restaurant’s layered flavors. The experience is refined, generous and highly polished, with the chef closely involved in service. It will appeal to diners seeking an elegant, ingredient-driven tasting experience in a grand setting.

name: Prévelle
description: Prévelle offers a modern, seasonal tasting experience shaped by Romain Meder’s plant-forward approach and his focus on local Île-de-France produce. Vegetables take center stage, with poultry, fish, and shellfish used to complement the menu, all handled with notable technical precision and bold seasoning. The setting is calm and understated, with clean lines and an open-kitchen element that adds a sense of quiet theatre. It should appeal to diners who enjoy refined, ingredient-led cooking with a strong emphasis on seasonality, depth of flavour, and a polished but relaxed atmosphere.

name: Pur'
address: Park Hyatt Paris-Vendôme, 5 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France
description: At Pur’, Jean-François Rouquette presents contemporary cuisine in a fresh, design-led dining room at the Park Hyatt Paris-Vendôme. The cooking is precise and inventive, with a clear emphasis on excellent produce and refined combinations. Dishes may feature multiple preparations of the same ingredient, as seen in lamb from the Pyrenees paired with thoughtful vegetable and seaweed accents. The setting feels polished and modern, making it a strong choice for diners who appreciate creative fine dining with technical finesse and a calm, elegant atmosphere.

name: Qui Plume la Lune
description: Qui Plume la Lune is a one-star French restaurant in Paris’s 11th arrondissement, offering a creative seasonal menu built around carefully selected ingredients. The dining room combines warm, elegant touches with two contrasting interiors, one pared back with stone and natural materials, the other more colorful and decorative. Dishes are artfully presented and balanced in flavor, with a style that feels refined yet inviting. It should appeal to diners looking for contemporary French cooking in a romantic setting, with thoughtful plating and a polished, ingredient-led approach.

name: Quinsou
description: Quinsou is a one-star French restaurant in Paris 6e that pairs artisanal cooking with a relaxed, vintage setting. Opposite Ferrandi, it offers a warm, intimate atmosphere with cement tiles and a zinc bar, and a style that emphasizes simplicity and precision. The kitchen focuses on market-fresh ingredients and seasonal dishes, with a lunchtime menu noted for strong value. Signature preparations such as duck and foie gras pâté en croûte suggest careful technique and balance. It should appeal to diners seeking refined, ingredient-led cooking in a small, unpretentious room.

name: Relais Louis XIII
description: Relais Louis XIII offers classic French cooking in a historic Paris setting, with an ornate dining room that blends old-world details such as stone, woodwork, stained glass, and wall hangings with a few contemporary touches. The atmosphere feels formal and occasion-worthy, matching a kitchen devoted to traditional Gallic cuisine. Led by a chef known for championing French culinary classicism, the restaurant will appeal to diners who appreciate refined, heritage-driven cooking in a setting with a strong sense of history and ceremony.

name: Restaurant Guy Savoy
description: Restaurant Guy Savoy is a Michelin-starred French dining destination on the Quai de Conti in Paris’s 6th arrondissement. Set within the Monnaie de Paris, it offers a refined setting that suits a special-occasion meal. The restaurant is associated with high-end contemporary French cuisine and a polished fine-dining experience. Its reputation and central Paris location make it appealing to diners seeking an elegant, celebratory lunch or dinner in a landmark setting. It is especially well suited to guests looking for a classic luxury restaurant experience in the city.

name: Restaurant H
description: This intimate Paris 4th arrondissement restaurant offers contemporary cooking in a discreet, elegant setting with fewer than 20 seats. The single set menu focuses on premium seasonal produce and local ingredients, with dishes that are inventive, precise, and carefully balanced. Signature plates such as razor clams with parsley hint at a kitchen that pairs roasted and tangy notes with confidence. A chic yet cosy atmosphere and an optional food-and-wine pairing make it well suited to diners seeking a refined, small-scale tasting experience.

name: Restaurant Le Meurice
address: Le Meurice, 228 rue de Rivoli, 75001 Paris, France
description: Set inside an iconic luxury hotel opposite the Jardin des Tuileries, Le Meurice pairs French fine dining with a distinctly opulent setting. Crystal chandeliers, gilded ceilings, and a Versailles-inspired decor create a grand, polished atmosphere. The kitchen emphasizes tasting menus in small portions, balancing French tradition with creative touches. Desserts are a particular highlight, adding to the restaurant’s refined appeal. With its elegant style and high-end service, it is well suited to diners seeking a classic Parisian luxury experience and a memorable special-occasion meal.

name: Septime
description: Septime is a Michelin-starred Paris restaurant known for modern French cooking with a fresh, confident, and inventive spirit. Set on Rue de Charonne, it has a spacious, loft-like dining room with untreated wood tables, metal rafters, and a distinctly contemporary, industrial feel. The atmosphere is stylish and energetic, matched by polished service. It suits diners looking for a hip, trend-aware address that still takes food seriously. Reservations can be hard to secure, which adds to its sought-after appeal.

name: Shabour
description: Shabour is a Michelin-starred Israeli restaurant in Paris’s 2nd arrondissement, set in a 17th-century building near rue Saint-Denis and rue Montorgueil. The room has a lively feel, with industrial touches, visible ductwork and subdued lighting. The cooking is generous and inventive, drawing on Mediterranean influences while keeping a fresh, modern edge. Dishes may combine familiar ingredients in unexpected ways, making the menu appealing to diners who enjoy creative, flavor-driven cuisine in an energetic setting. It suits those looking for a distinctive meal with personality and a strong sense of place.

name: Sola
description: Sola offers a refined Franco-Japanese dining experience in Paris, led by chef Kosuke Nabeta. The cooking combines Japanese precision with French produce, using techniques such as smoking, drying, marinating, and fermenting to create balanced, polished dishes. The setting is equally distinctive: pale wood, a coffered ceiling, solid doors, and a vaulted cellar with a Japanese-inspired dining platform. Its atmosphere feels calm and carefully composed, with a strong sense of contrast between Paris and Japan. It should appeal to diners who enjoy thoughtful, technique-driven cuisine in an intimate, characterful room.

name: Solstice
description: Solstice is an intimate, elegant Paris dining room led by Éric Trochon, a Meilleur Ouvrier de France and Michelin-starred chef. The cooking is contemporary and restrained, with a minimalist menu that includes an omakase option and dishes shaped by Asian accents. Expect precise plates such as beef with mushrooms and Korean-inspired seasoning, served in a refined setting with Brazilian rosewood furnishings and artwork by a Korean artist. A counter with views into the kitchen adds to the experience. It should appeal to diners who enjoy modern, detail-driven cuisine in a calm, polished atmosphere.

name: Substance
description: Substance is a contemporary French restaurant in Paris’s 16th arrondissement, led by a duo with a lively, modern approach. The cooking leans seasonal and highly local, with Italian influences woven through the menu and a strong emphasis on vibrant sauces and bright herbal or citrus accents. A fully plant-based menu is available and sounds especially appealing, while signature dishes include a reworked cacio e pepe with tomato and zaatar. The atmosphere is fashionable and the wine list focuses on mostly organic and natural bottles, making it a fit for diners who enjoy inventive, ingredient-driven cuisine.

name: Sushi B
description: Sushi B is an intimate Japanese restaurant beside Louvois Square, with just eight seats and a polished, serene setting of marble, upholstered chairs and fine glassware. The focus is on carefully prepared sushi, maki and seasonal dishes made with very fresh ingredients and precise technique. Flavours are balanced rather than forceful, with thoughtful pairings and clean textures. This is a place for diners who appreciate meticulous craftsmanship, understated elegance and a compact, chef-driven experience in central Paris.

name: Sushi Shunei
description: Perched on Montmartre’s hill, Sushi Shunei offers an elegant, minimalist setting with light wood, a long counter and a striking origami-inspired ceiling. The restaurant focuses on omakase, with two menu versions chosen at booking, and showcases precise nigiri, carefully selected fish and subtle seasoning. Its style is refined yet restrained, with attentive, unobtrusive hospitality that reflects Japanese dining traditions. This is a strong choice for diners seeking a polished sushi experience in a calm, design-led room, especially those who appreciate craftsmanship and a tasting menu built around seasonal fish.

name: Sushi Yoshinaga
description: Sushi Yoshinaga offers an intimate omakase experience built around precision and restraint. The room is carefully composed, with Japanese ceramics, cedar trays, maple panelling, and subdued lighting creating a calm setting for just a handful of diners. At the counter, the chef focuses on exceptional fish, especially tuna, prepared with exacting attention to texture, temperature, and seasoning. House marinades, soy sauces, and a few warm opening dishes add personal touches to the sequence. Best suited to diners seeking a highly refined, quietly theatrical sushi meal.

name: Table Bruno Verjus
description: Table Bruno Verjus offers a refined French tasting-menu experience built around exceptional ingredients and a clear respect for producers. The chef’s approach is restrained and ingredient-led, with dishes made to order and sauces and garnishes used to enhance rather than mask flavor. Guests sit at the counter on comfortable stools, creating an intimate, conversational atmosphere. The daily changing menu adds a sense of discovery and seasonality. It should appeal to diners who value precise cooking, thoughtful sourcing, and a more personal form of fine dining in Paris.

name: Taillevent
address: Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris, France
description: Taillevent is a Paris institution devoted to French classicism, set in a former 19th-century private mansion that creates a hushed, cocooned atmosphere. The cooking emphasizes tradition, with select dishes finished tableside and served in two courses, alongside enduring classics such as crêpes Suzette. Under the Gardinier family, it offers a polished, formal experience anchored by a notably impressive wine list. It will appeal to diners seeking an elegant, old-school expression of French fine dining, especially those who value ritual, heritage, and meticulous service.

name: Tomy & Co
description: Tomy & Co is a Michelin-starred French restaurant in Paris 7e that blends a gourmet-bistro spirit with a modern, ingredient-led approach. Chef Tomy Gousset brings a confident, playful style shaped by experience in notable kitchens, while keeping the focus on local sourcing and clear flavors. The atmosphere suggests an unpretentious yet polished dining room where simplicity meets sophistication. It should appeal to diners looking for contemporary French cooking with personality, especially those who enjoy a relaxed bistro feel paired with refined execution.

name: Tour d'Argent
address: 15 quai de la Tournelle, 75005 Paris, France
description: A historic Paris address with a contemporary edge, Tour d’Argent pairs classic French refinement with modern touches. The dining room is defined by sweeping views over the Seine and Notre-Dame, an open kitchen, and a striking ceiling detail that adds drama to the setting. The cooking balances tradition and innovation, with carefully crafted sauces, updated house classics, and tableside finishing that brings extra theatre. An exceptional wine cellar and the rooftop terrace further enhance the experience. It will appeal to diners seeking a polished, scenic meal with strong culinary heritage and a sense of occasion.

name: Trente-Trois
address: Maison Villeroy, 33 rue Jean-Goujon, 75008 Paris, France
description: Trente-Trois is a discreet one-star French restaurant in Paris 8, set within a Belle Époque dining room in a hotel near the Champs-Élysées. The atmosphere is plush and refined, with a quietly luxurious feel. The kitchen favors a concise menu built around premium produce, seasonal ingredients, and precise cooking. Dishes are described as subtle and contemporary, with particular attention to balanced flavors, sauces, and jus. It should appeal to diners looking for polished, understated fine dining in an elegant setting.

name: Vaisseau
description: Vaisseau is a Michelin-starred creative restaurant in Paris 11e where chef Adrien Cachot showcases a playful, highly personal style. The setting is minimalist and intimate, keeping the focus on a carte blanche menu built around unexpected pairings, less conventional ingredients, and occasional surf-and-turf combinations. Dishes may feature offal, seafood, and sharp contrasts in texture and flavor, all presented with a sense of surprise. It should appeal to diners who enjoy inventive cooking, a compact atmosphere, and a meal that feels adventurous from start to finish.

name: Virtus
description: Virtus is a two-star contemporary restaurant in Paris’s 12th arrondissement, near the Marché d’Aligre. Its vintage, Art Deco-accented dining room feels like a refined Parisian apartment, creating an intimate and polished setting. In the kitchen, seasonal produce and precise technique drive a menu built around carefully composed dishes and aromatic sauces. Expect thoughtful, technically assured cooking with a strong sense of balance and detail, from fish to poultry preparations. It should appeal to diners who value elegant surroundings, exacting craftsmanship, and modern French cuisine with a highly disciplined touch.

name: Yoshinori
description: Yoshinori offers refined contemporary cuisine shaped by a Japanese sensibility, presented through single set menus that emphasize elegance and precision. Dishes may pair ingredients such as veal, cockles, cauliflower, monkfish, mushrooms, or pigeon with thoughtful accents and polished presentation. The dining room is intimate and distinctive, with exposed stone, white beams, Japanese-style woodwork, designer lighting, and crisp table settings. In the busy Latin Quarter, it stands out as a calm, polished address for diners seeking a carefully composed tasting experience and attentive service. A lunchtime set menu adds an accessible option.

name: Ze Kitchen Galerie
description: Ze Kitchen Galerie is a long-running Michelin-starred address in Paris 6e where French cooking is shaped by Southeast Asian flavors. Chef William Ledeuil’s style is creative and precise, using herbs, roots, and spices such as galangal, turmeric, ginger, and wasabi to lift dishes built around fish, broths, pasta, and classic French foundations. The room is pared back yet lively, with designer furnishings, colorful art, and a window onto the kitchen. It should appeal to diners who enjoy inventive, flavor-driven cuisine in a polished but relaxed setting.

name: Zostera
description: Zostera is a Michelin-starred dining room in Paris’s 16th arrondissement, set in an elegant, tastefully decorated space. Chef Julien Dumas presents creative, marine-led cuisine shaped by a strong interest in the Atlantic and a careful approach to sourcing. The menu highlights delicate seafood, precise cooking, and well-judged seasoning, alongside vegetables that receive equal attention. Expect refined dishes such as Arctic char, scallops, and seasonal produce in a polished setting with attentive service. It should appeal to diners looking for a technically assured, seafood-focused tasting experience.

name: Épicure
address: Le Bristol, 112 rue du Faubourg-Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris, France
description: Set within Le Bristol, Épicure offers a highly polished fine-dining experience in a luxurious Paris setting. The room is formal and elegant, with Louis XVI furnishings, mirrors, and tall windows overlooking the garden. In the kitchen, the approach is contemporary and restrained, favoring light, refined preparations that emphasize purity of flavor without sacrificing generosity. Expect excellent ingredients, precise cooking, carefully balanced sauces, house-made bread, and meticulous plating. It will appeal to diners seeking classic luxury, technical finesse, and a serene, stylish atmosphere for a special occasion.

name: Ōrtensia
description: Ōrtensia is a one-star French restaurant in Paris with a subtle Japanese influence, shaping a menu that feels precise and seasonal. The cooking is said to shift with the weather, suggesting a thoughtful, changing approach rather than a fixed repertoire. Inside, the mood is contemporary and minimalist, with light neutral tones, wood, and mirrors that lend a sense of openness. It should appeal to diners looking for refined French cuisine with a restrained, modern setting and a distinctive cross-cultural touch. Reservations are available online only.
